notes: GQA (grouped query attention) with 64 Q heads, 4 KV heads
Identity activation preserves signed values for softmax
Rate-coded LIF with T>=64 achieves <2% relative error
Residual stream integrity maintained via ANN-equivalent path

moe_router.method: LAS identity activation (expert selection from signed logits)
notes: Router: 2048 -> 256 (top-8 selection)
Identity activation critical: signed logits determine ranking
ANN-equivalent routing used for selection (spiking for energy)
norm_topk_prob normalises expert combination weights

moe_expert_ffn.method: LAS relu activation on gate/up/down (SwiGLU issue)
blocking_issues: SwiGLU gate uses SiLU (x * sigmoid(x)), not ReLU -- rate-coded LIF only approximates ReLU natively
Signed intermediate activations from SiLU need two-channel encoding (positive + negative spike trains)
gate_proj * up_proj element-wise multiply has no direct spiking equivalent -- requires temporal correlation coding
research_paths: Two-channel signed rate code (2x spike overhead, halves energy gain)
Surrogate SiLU via shifted ReLU: silu(x) ~ relu(x+0.5) - 0.25 (rough, ~8% error at boundary)
Hybrid: keep SwiGLU gate in ANN, convert up/down to spiking (pragmatic, ~60% of expert params converted)
Temporal correlation coding for element-wise multiply (active research, no proven LAS-compatible method yet)
notes: Each expert: 2048 -> 512 -> 2048 (SwiGLU)
Only 8/256 experts active per token
down_proj (inter -> hidden) is straightforward ReLU conversion
gate_proj output feeds SiLU -- the conversion bottleneck

shared_expert.method: Same SwiGLU issues as MoE experts
notes: Shared expert: 2048 -> 512 -> 2048
Always active (not gated by router)
Same SwiGLU conversion challenges as regular experts
Embedding is a lookup, not amenable to spiking conversion
lm_head logits need full precision for sampling
Spike-to-MAC ratio < 1 means fewer spike events than MAC ops
Each spike = 1 addition; each MAC = 1 multiply + 1 add
Actual sparsity depends on activation distribution (ReLU helps)
With T=128, quantisation error < 0.0078 per neuron

caveats: Energy model assumes 45nm CMOS; modern 5nm would scale all values
Spike ops count assumes mean activation 0.5 (uniform distribution)
Real activation sparsity (post-ReLU) would reduce spike count
SwiGLU gate path not fully convertible — hybrid energy mix
Does not include memory access energy (often dominant)
Neuromorphic estimate assumes dedicated hardware (Loihi 2 class)

conversion_summary.verdict: Attention layers (GQA projections) and MoE routers are fully convertible via LAS with identity activation. MoE expert FFNs are partially convertible: down_proj is straightforward, but SwiGLU gate (SiLU activation + element-wise multiply) has no direct spiking equivalent. Recommended approach: hybrid conversion keeping SwiGLU gate in ANN domain, converting up/down projections to spiking. This yields ~60% expert parameter conversion with predictable energy savings.

research_questions.question: Can SiLU be approximated by shifted ReLU in rate code?
Does two-channel signed encoding preserve MoE quality?
research_questions.notes: 2x spike overhead; needs empirical validation on MoE

research_questions.question: What is the minimum T for <1% attention error at 35B scale?
research_questions.notes: Theory: T=128 gives error bound 0.0078; empirical validation needs weight samples

research_questions.question: Can temporal correlation coding handle element-wise multiply?
research_questions.notes: Active research area; no proven LAS-compatible method
